an1.170:0.2: 13. The Chapter on the One Person
an1.170:1.1: "One person, *bhikkhus*, arises in the world for the welfare of the many, for the happiness of the many, out of compassion for the world, for the good, the welfare, and the happiness of gods and humans.
an1.170:1.3: The Tathāgata, the *arahant*, the Perfectly Enlightened One.
an1.170:1.4: This, *bhikkhus*, is the one person who arises in the world for the welfare of the many, for the happiness of the many, out of compassion for the world, for the good, the welfare, and the happiness of gods and humans."
an1.171:1.1: "The appearance of one person, *bhikkhus*, is rare in the world.
an1.171:1.3: The Tathāgata, the *arahant*, the Perfectly Enlightened One.
an1.171:1.4: The appearance of this one person, *bhikkhus*, is rare in the world."
an1.172:1.1: "One person, *bhikkhus*, arises in the world as an extraordinary human being.
an1.172:1.3: The Tathāgata, the *arahant*, the Perfectly Enlightened One.
an1.172:1.4: This, *bhikkhus*, is the one person who arises in the world as an extraordinary human being."
an1.173:1.1: "The passing away of one person, *bhikkhus*, is a cause of grief to the many.
an1.173:1.3: The Tathāgata, the *arahant*, the Perfectly Enlightened One.
an1.173:1.4: The passing away of this one person, *bhikkhus*, is a cause of grief to the many."
an1.174:1.1: "One person, *bhikkhus*, arises in the world who is without a second, without a companion, without equal, without counterpart, without parallel, without peer, incomparable, unmatched, the foremost of two-footed beings.
an1.174:1.3: The Tathāgata, the *arahant*, the Perfectly Enlightened One.
an1.174:1.4: This, *bhikkhus*, is the one person who arises in the world who is without a second, without a companion, without equal, without counterpart, without parallel, without peer, incomparable, unmatched, the foremost of two-footed beings."
an1.175-186:1.1: "With the appearance of one person, *bhikkhus*, there appears a great eye, there appears a great light, there appears a great radiance, there appears the manifestation of the six unsurpassable things, there is the realization of the four analytical knowledges, there is penetration of the many elements, there is penetration of the diverse elements, there is the realization of the fruit of knowledge and liberation, there is the realization of the fruit of stream-entry, there is the realization of the fruit of once-returning, there is the realization of the fruit of non-returning, there is the realization of the fruit of arahantship.
an1.175-186:1.3: The Tathāgata, the *arahant*, the Perfectly Enlightened One.
an1.175-186:1.4: With the appearance of this one person, *bhikkhus*, there appears a great eye, there appears a great light, there appears a great radiance, there appears the manifestation of the six unsurpassable things, there is the realization of the four analytical knowledges, there is penetration of the many elements, there is penetration of the diverse elements, there is the realization of the fruit of knowledge and liberation, there is the realization of the fruit of stream-entry, there is the realization of the fruit of once-returning, there is the realization of the fruit of non-returning, there is the realization of the fruit of arahantship."
an1.187:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, even one other person who so rightly keeps in motion the unsurpassed wheel of *dhamma* set in motion by the Tathāgata as does Sāriputta.
an1.187:1.2: Sāriputta, *bhikkhus*, rightly keeps in motion the unsurpassed wheel of *dhamma* set in motion by the Tathāgata."
an1.187:1.3: The Chapter on the One Person, the thirteenth.
